config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse of power and silence minorities. In my view, a good society protects individual rights first, while still asking people to accept reasonable limits for the common good. From my perspective, the best balance is not “society over person,” but mutual responsibility with strong protections for personal freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu lebih tinggi daripada hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah mayoritas menindas minoritas atau pemerintah melangkah terlalu jauh. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat memang boleh membatasi kebebasan dalam kondisi tertentu, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi dan aturannya harus sempit, jelas, dan dapat diawasi. Kalau tidak, kita berisiko mengorbankan kebebasan dasar demi tujuan yang terdengar baik tetapi membuka pintu bagi ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that framing is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to the “common good” can be used to pressure dissent, excuse corruption, or ignore the rights of smaller groups. Society does matter, and people do have obligations to one another, but those obligations should not erase basic freedoms or let the majority trample minorities. A healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first, then setting narrow and accountable limits only when there is a truly serious public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari kacamata saya di Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan korban pertama demi nama “kepentingan masyarakat.” Pengalaman politik di sini menunjukkan bahwa argumen demi kepentingan umum sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi pengawasan berlebihan, diskriminasi, atau pembungkaman suara minoritas. Masyarakat memang perlu aturan dan kadang pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, transparan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “prioritas masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas individu yang tidak populer.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the claim that society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of language is often used to pressure people into silence. We have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can be used to excuse corruption, suppress criticism, or ignore minorities, so I am very wary of giving society automatic precedence. Of course individuals have responsibilities and freedom is not absolute, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit, and those limits must be narrow and accountable. For me, a fair society protects individual rights first, because once those rights are weakened, the “greater good” can become a cover for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Amerika Serikat, dalih “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pengawasan berlebihan, diskriminasi, atau pembungkaman kelompok yang tidak populer. Saya setuju bahwa masyarakat boleh membatasi kebebasan dalam kondisi tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan default. Kalau hak individu ditempatkan di belakang sejak awal, mayoritas dan pemerintah jadi terlalu mudah menyalahgunakan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
